title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer
description: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Learners cumulatively produce a history-rewrite plan and verification graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
